summary: The Borodin–Kostochka conjecture remains open for general graphs with $\Delta \ge 9$. Since the problem was posted, the conjecture has been verified for several hereditary graph classes ($(P_5,\text{gem})$-free, $(P_6,\text{apple,torch})$-free, and others), and the unconditional degree threshold has been reduced from Reed's $\Delta_0 \le 10^{14}$ to $\Delta \ge 5.2\times10^9$ in 2026; a companion 2026 paper also establishes the analogue for correspondence (DP) coloring at $\Delta \ge 3\times10^9$.

since_posted.title: Coloring $(P_5, \text{gem})$-free graphs with $\Delta-1$ colors
since_posted.authors: Daniel W. Cranston, Hudson Lafayette, Landon Rabern
since_posted.claim: Proves the Borodin–Kostochka conjecture for $(P_5,\text{gem})$-free graphs: if $\Delta(G)\ge 9$ and $\omega(G)\le\Delta(G)-1$ then $\chi(G)\le\Delta(G)-1$.

since_posted.title: Validity of Borodin and Kostochka Conjecture for classes of graphs without a single, forbidden subgraph on 5 vertices
since_posted.claim: Verifies the conjecture for $(P_4\cup K_1)$-free, $P_5$-free, Chair-free, and graphs with dense neighborhoods.

since_posted.title: Borodin-Kostochka conjecture for a family of $P_6$-free graphs
since_posted.claim: Proves the conjecture for $(P_6,\text{apple,torch})$-free graphs, extending the known result for $(P_5,C_4)$-free graphs.

since_posted.title: Coloring some $(P_6,C_4)$-free graphs with $\Delta-1$ colors
since_posted.authors: Ran Chen, Di Wu, Xiaowen Zhang
since_posted.claim: Proves the conjecture for $(P_6,C_4,H)$-free graphs where $H\in\{K_7,C_5^+\}$.

since_posted.title: On graphs with chromatic number and maximum degree both equal to nine
since_posted.claim: Proves structural results in support of the $\Delta=9$ base case of the conjecture, showing that vertex-critical graphs satisfying certain conditions must contain $K_3\vee\overline{K_6}$.

since_posted.title: On the Borodin–Kostochka conjecture for graphs with large maximum degree
since_posted.authors: Feng Liu, Shuang Sun, Yan Wang
since_posted.claim: Reduces the unconditional threshold: every graph with $\Delta\ge 5.2\times10^9$ and $\omega(G)<\Delta$ satisfies $\chi(G)\le\Delta-1$, improving Reed's 1999 bound of $\Delta_0\le 10^{14}$.

since_posted.title: On Borodin-Kostochka conjecture for correspondence coloring
since_posted.authors: Zděněk Dvořák, Ross J. Kang, David Mikšaník
since_posted.claim: Proves that for $\Delta\ge 3\times10^9$, every graph satisfies $\chi_{\mathrm{DP}}(G)\le\max(\omega(G),\Delta-1)$, establishing the correspondence-coloring analogue of the conjecture for large degree.

search_queries: Borodin-Kostochka conjecture chromatic number maximum degree proof 2013 2014 2015 2016 2017 2018 2019 2020
Cranston Rabern "Borodin-Kostochka" coloring graph Delta-1 colors arXiv
Brooks theorem strengthening chromatic number Delta-1 coloring clique number conjecture recent progress
notes: The conjecture is definitively still open in general. A ScienceDirect paper on the list-chromatic version (S0012365X22005064) and a paper on K_{1,3}-bar-free graphs (S0166218X2400266X) returned HTTP 403 and could not be verified; they are not cited. The arXiv paper 2101.01354 by Dhurandhar is not peer-reviewed and should be treated with appropriate caution. The 2026 papers (2603.16670 and 2603.14427) are preprints not yet journal-published as of this review date.
